title: verbascum trapifolium var. flabellifolium
intro: Verbascum trapifolium var. flabellifolium, also known as Mullein, is a biennial plant in the figwort family that is characterized by its large, lobed leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Mullein have been used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, which can help to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Mullein include reducing inflammation, improving sleep, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for a range of conditions. Mullein can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
